title: Thor/Party Prince Thor
content:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Thor {{!}} Party Prince Thor}} {{Otheruses|Thor}} {{CharHub|Thor|Thor}} {{AltUniverse|the "[[Party Prince Thor Universe]]"|3=Characters|4=1}} {{Character |name = Thor |image = Party Thor infobox.png |real name = Thor Odinson{{Ref|WI107}} |alias = {{Alias |codenames = Party Prince Thor{{Ref|WI107}}}} |species = [[Asgardian]] |gender = Male |title = God of Thunder
Crown Prince of [[Asgard]] |citizenship = {{Citizenship|ASG1}} |affiliation = {{Affiliation|ASGPPT}}
[[Guardians of the Multiverse]] (formerly) |tv series = ''[[What If...?]]'' (2 episodes) |docuseries = ''[[Legends]]''
*''[[Guardians of the Multiverse (episode)|Guardians of the Multiverse]]'' |game = {{Infobox List|{{Collapse|''[[What If...? – An Immersive Story]]'' (depiction)}}}} |voice actor = [[Chris Hemsworth]] |status = Alive}} {{Quote|My [[Odin/Party Prince Thor|father]] has ruled [[Asgard]] for centuries. And, look, the most interesting thing that he’s ever done is [[Odinsleep|fall asleep]]. He only cares about duty and blah-blah-blah-blah responsibility. Well, I'm not gonna be like him, right? The boring king? Uh-uh.|Thor|What If... Thor Were an Only Child?}} Prince '''Thor Odinson''' is the son of King [[Odin/Party Prince Thor|Odin]] and Queen [[Frigga/Party Prince Thor|Frigga]] who grew up as an only child and grew to be a rambunctious adult who threw multiple parties throughout the galaxy. ==Biography== {{MD|Thor Odinson (Earth-72124)}} ===[[Party on Midgard]]=== ====Celebration with Humanity==== {{Quote|Citizens of [[Earth|Midgard]]... your dull lives are about to come to an end. Prepare yourselves... for the party prince!|Thor|What If... Thor Were an Only Child?}} After [[Odin/Party Prince Thor|Odin]] fell in the [[Odinsleep]] and [[Frigga/Party Prince Thor|Frigga]] left to visit her sisters to celebrate the solstice, Thor was left under [[Heimdall/Party Prince Thor|Heimdall]]'s supervision. However, Thor, along with [[Sif/Party Prince Thor|Sif]] and the [[Warriors Three/Party Prince Thor|Warriors Three]], secretly escaped Heimdall through [[Odin's Vault]] to travel to [[Earth]]. Thor, Sif, and the Warriors Three arrived via the [[Bifrost Bridge]] in [[Las Vegas]], [[Nevada]] startling the tourists. Thor announced himself as the "Party Prince" to ease the tourists' alarm and started a party. Thor then invited extraterrestrial beings from across the universe to attend. [[File:Party Thor First Look.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Thor dances with [[Jane Foster/Party Prince Thor|Jane Foster]]]] Thor met with a few [[Skrulls]], who he asked if they could transform into his appearance, before he was greeted by [[Jane Foster/Party Prince Thor|Jane Foster]]. Foster wanted to ask him serious questions about his arrival, but was enticed by Thor to join his party. Thor introduced Foster to his friends and received tattoos with her. Later that night, Thor crashed in a nearby hotel room with [[Fandral/Party Prince Thor|Fandral]], [[Rocket/Party Prince Thor|Rocket]], [[Korg/Party Prince Thor|Korg]], and Foster before leaving for [[Paris]], [[France]]. In Paris, Thor met with [[Loki/Party Prince Thor|Loki]] and other [[Frost Giants]] and witnessed what they thought was a shooting star. However, it ended up being [[Captain Marvel/Party Prince Thor|Captain Marvel]].{{Ref|WI107}} ====[[Attack on Thor|Brawl with Captain Marvel]]==== [[File:Party Thor and Cap Marvel What If... 1x7 Disney+.png|{{AltFloat}}|Thor being confronted by [[Captain Marvel/Party Prince Thor|Captain Marvel]]]] {{Quote|Hey, Whitesnake. We need to chat."
"The name is Thor, with a "T" and an "R" and an "O" and a "H." But not in that order.|[[Captain Marvel/Party Prince Thor|Captain Marvel]] and Thor|What If... Thor Were an Only Child?}} Danvers demanded Thor to leave Earth and end his planet-wide party. Thor refused, calling her a party pooper, and engaged in a fight. After Thor got the upper hand using [[Mjølnir]] and blasted Danvers to the [[Stonehenge]], he arrived and out of petty spite, knocked the structures over. Danvers then struck him, sending him flying back into [[New Mexico]]. [[File:Thor vs. Carol Danvers.png|{{AltFloat}}|Thor fighting against [[Captain Marvel/Party Prince Thor|Captain Marvel]]]] In the desert, they fought with Thor trying to blast Danvers with lightning from Mjølnir, but she absorbed the energy using her powers. The two then engaged in aerial fighting and travelled across the world, landing back in Paris. Upon arriving, Thor placed Mjølnir on Danvers' chest, preventing her from getting up, and successfully defeating her.{{Ref|WI107}} ====Cleaning Up The Planet==== Afterwards, Thor traveled to [[Sydney]], [[Australia]] and was about to slide down the [[Sydney Opera House]] to impress his friends, when he was caught by Danvers who brought him to [[Siberia]]. As the two were about to fight again, [[Frigga/Party Prince Thor|Frigga]] appeared as a projection, informing him that Foster had contacted her. He tried to convince her he was [[Asgard|home]], but the passing polar bear revealed he was on Earth. He then told her he was there to study, but she said she would be coming to see his progress in his "studies". Now worried, Thor asked for Danvers' help in hiding his mess, but she suggested he start in the corners and clean out. Arriving back in Sydney, Thor told his friends, such as [[Drax/Party Prince Thor|Drax]] and [[Nebula/Party Prince Thor|Nebula]], that he needed their help to clean up, but they decided to leave instead. In a panic, Thor summoned his power and projected his voice to them telling them that his mother was on the way. This prompted his friends to begin a hastened and thorough attempt to clean up their mess and repair the damage that resulted from the blowout. By the time Frigga arrived, Thor was in [[Italy]] standing in front of the Warriors Three and a few others, pretending to be studying. Danvers arrived as well, and to his relief, she went along with the façade. Frigga began to believe him, until he summoned Mjølnir, revealing that it was covered in graffiti, beads, and a pair of boxers, exposing what happened. [[File:Jane and Thor plan a date.png|{{AltFloat}}|Thor plans a date with [[Jane Foster/Party Prince Thor|Jane Foster]]]] Afterwards, Thor travelled back to [[New Mexico]] and visited Foster at her van. He was then prompted by her to ask her out on a proper date. However, as they said goodbye, a [[Wormhole|portal]] opened up before them, with an [[Ultron Sentries|army of robots]] and their [[Infinity Ultron/Age of Ultron|leader]] wielding the six [[Infinity Stones]] walking through.{{Ref|WI107}} ===[[Battle for the Multiverse]]=== ====Invasion in Las Vegas==== [[File:Thor vs. Ultron Sentry (What If).jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Thor fights the [[Ultron Sentries]]]] {{Quote|We were about to go on a date! There were going to be flowers, and I was going to light candles, and I was going to shower and do my hair! Nobody messes with Vegas! Have at you! And you!|Thor to [[Ultron Sentries]]|What If... The Watcher Broke His Oath?}} The [[Ultron Sentries]] invaded Thor's universe and travelled to [[Las Vegas]], the area with a large population closest to the desert. Thor fought them off, defending Las Vegas, while complaining how they effectively ruined his date with Foster. After nearly defeating all of them, Thor was found by the [[Watcher]] and snatched out of his universe.{{Ref|WI109}} ====Joining the Guardians of the Multiverse==== [[File:Watcher and the Guardians of the Multiverse.png|{{AltFloat}}|Thor and the [[Guardians of the Multiverse]] being briefed by [[Watcher]]]] Thor was transported to a dimension outside of time and space constructed by [[Strange Supreme]] in the form of a bar. Thor then asked Strange for a beer and met the others present: [[Captain Carter/Super Soldier Peggy Carter|Captain Carter]], [[T'Challa/Ravager T'Challa|Star-Lord T'Challa]], Gamora, and [[Erik Killmonger/Killmonger's War|Erik Killmonger]]. Thor was then startled again after the Watcher appeared abruptly before them. The Watcher explained to them Ultron's quest to conquer the [[Multiverse]] and Strange explained the [[Infinity Stones]] to them. Afterwards, the Watcher referred to them as the [[Guardians of the Multiverse]]. [[File:Doctor Strange Supreme and Party Thor.png|{{AltFloat}}|Thor witnesses [[Strange Supreme]] summoning [[zombies]]]] After making a plan, Thor and the Guardians were transported to another universe on an uninhabited planet. While there, Gamora asked Thor if he trusted Killmonger, in which he replied that he trusted everyone. Strange then provided drinks for them and Thor used [[Mjølnir]] in excitement, inadvertently alerting [[Infinity Ultron/Age of Ultron|Ultron]] to their location. Strange supplied Thor and the others with a protection spell as Ultron broke into the universe. Thor attacked Ultron using his lightning but Ultron absorbed the energy and proceeded to try to kill them. As the other Guardians escaped, Thor and Strange remained behind, and Thor witnessed Strange conjure a [[Inter-Dimensional Portal]] from another universe, summoning [[Zombies|zombies]] to overwhelm Ultron. Thor and Strange then escape through the portal to Ultron's home universe, where they and the others meet [[Black Widow/Age of Ultron|Black Widow]], who agrees to fight with them against Ultron, when he then arrives. Thor and the others fight Ultron, at which he blasts Ultron with a massive amount on him, but to no avail. After the [[Infinity Crusher]] was used on Ultron, Thor asked if it was over, but Ultron then appeared. When Ultron was unleashing the full power of the Infinity Stones to kill Strange, Thor was barley able to keep his ground due to the amount of power but was saved when Black Widow was able to shoot an arrow containing Arnim Zola into Ultron's eye where Zola effortlessly killed Ultron and stopping his reign of terror. After the fight Thor recovered from the shocking blow. [[File:Guardians of the Multiverse.png|{{AltFloat}}|Thor and the [[Guardians of the Multiverse|Guardians]] upon successfully defeating [[Infinity Ultron/Age of Ultron|Ultron]], [[Arnim Zola/Age of Ultron|Zola]] and [[Erik Killmonger/Killmonger's War|Killmonger]]]] However tensions resurfaced when [[Erik Killmonger/Killmonger's War|Killmonger]] used Ultron's [[Infinity Armor]] to acquire the [[Infinity Stones]]. As Thor and the rest of the Guardians prepared to face him, Killmonger used the Stones to unleash their true power against them. But Zola, who now has complete control over Ultron's former body to try and take the stones from himself. This resulted in both of them getting into a deadlock for the stones while Strange encased them into a pocket dimension. With the Multiverse secured, Thor and the rest of the Guardians retuned to the pub pocket dimension to celebrate their success.{{Ref|WI109}} ====Returning to his Universe==== Following the battle, [[Watcher]] congratulated Thor and the [[Guardians of the Multiverse]] for saving the [[Multiverse]] from [[Infinity Ultron/Age of Ultron|Ultron]] and said that the pub's door would bring them back to their home universes at the exact moment they left. Thor crossed the door and, when he came back, he reunited with [[Jane Foster/Party Prince Thor|Jane Foster]] and they joyfully hugged each other.{{Ref|WI109}} ====Journey with the Guardians==== Thor returned to reassemble with the other [[Guardians of the Multiverse]] and took a group photo, which included new members of the team. However, he eventually left the team.{{Ref|WI307}} ==Powers and Abilities== ===Powers=== {{Quote|You're Thor? As in Thor, the Norse god of thunder?"
"I don't know anything about horse gods, but I do know how to bring the thunder.|[[Jane Foster/Party Prince Thor|Jane Foster]] and Thor|What If... Thor Were an Only Child?}} *'''Asgardian Physiology''': As an [[Asgardian]], Thor possesses incredible supernatural powers and superhuman attributes far surpassing many other species in the universe. **'''Superhuman Strength''': Thor is incredibly strong, as he could strike [[Captain Marvel/Party Prince Thor|Captain Marvel]] from [[France]] to the [[United Kingdom]] and topple [[Stonehenge]] like dominos with just one finger. He could also trade blows with Captain Marvel, whose punches have been described by Fury to be equal to ten [[Atomic Bomb|nuclear bombs]]. He also straightened the [[Leaning Tower of Pisa]]. **'''Superhuman Durability''': Thor is incredibly durable, capable of taking attacks from Captain Marvel with no visible injury, including getting punched from United Kingdom to [[United States of America|America]] and hurled into a mountain. **'''Superhuman Speed''': Thor possesses incredible superhuman speed, capable of keeping up with Captain Marvel throughout their entire fight. **'''Superhuman Agility''': Thor's agility, dexterity, balance, and bodily coordination make him a great warrior for battle. He moves with incredible grace and speed despite his considerable size and body density. **'''Superhuman Stamina''': Thor's advanced musculature is considerably more efficient than that of a human and most other [[Asgardians]]. As a result, his muscles produce far fewer fatigue toxins during physical activity compared to those of humans and most other members of his race. He showed no sign of exhaustion even after partying for extended periods of time and his fight against Captain Marvel. **'''Superhuman Reflexes''': Thor can react and dodge objects traveling at high speeds. **'''Regenerative Healing Factor''': Despite his astounding resistance to injury, it is still possible to injure Thor. However, due to his Asgardian physiology, Thor is able to heal at a rate significantly faster than a normal human and even much faster than most Asgardians. **'''Longevity''': Despite being over a thousand years old, Thor has the physical appearance of a young human adult. *'''[[Mjølnir]] Capabilities''': ''To be added'' **'''Electricity Generation''': Thor is capable of summoning lightning using [[Mjølnir]]. He used it against Captain Marvel and then to charge up a city's power grid after [[Surtur/Party Prince Thor|Surtur]] accidentally made it lose power. **'''Storm Manipulation''': Thor is able to control the weather using Mjølnir. He summoned a massive storm in order to catch the attention of his guests and convince them to help clean up his party. **'''Energy Projection''': With Mjølnir, Thor could project blasts, waves, and bolts of electrical energy of various sizes and intensities. **'''Weight Manipulation''': ''To be added'' **'''Flight''': Thor is capable of flying at high-speeds using Mjølnir. **'''Weapon Calling''': Thor can summon Mjølnir to his hand wherever it is, even from great distances, as shown when he summoned it from [[Stonehenge]] when he was at [[United States of America|America]]. ===Former Powers=== *'''Protection Spell''': [[File:Thor-Protection Spell3.jpg|thumb|250px|Thor receiving a protection spell]] During the Battle for the Multiverse, [[Strange Supreme]] summoned a [[Guardian Vishanti]] protection spell and placed it on his [[Guardians of the Multiverse|teammates]]. However, this spell wore off after several hits from [[Infinity Ultron/Age of Ultron|Ultron]]. **'''Physical Augmentation''': The protection spell augmented his condition, as he was capable of grabbing Ultron and slamming him into the ground. **'''Weapon Enhancement''': The protection spell augmented Mjølnir, and Thor allowed his hammer to injure Infinity Ultron during the battle. ===Abilities=== {{Quote|Smooth move, bro. You just--"
"Hammerang. Get it?|[[Captain Marvel/Party Prince Thor|Captain Marvel]] and Thor|What If... Thor Were an Only Child?}} *'''Master Combatant''': [[File:Captain Marvel vs. Thor.png|thumb|250px|Thor fighting against [[Captain Marvel/Party Prince Thor|Captain Marvel]]]] Thor is a highly skilled combatant, able to go toe-to-toe with [[Captain Marvel/Party Prince Thor|Captain Marvel]] in combat as well [[Infinity Ultron/Age of Ultron|Ultron]]. *'''Hammer Mastery''': Thor can effectively use [[Mjølnir]] in his fights, as shown when he battled Captain Marvel, eventually getting the upper hand. *'''Expert Marksman''': Thor is a highly skilled marksman, though his marksmanship is best shown in throwing his hammer. He is able to throw them with extreme accuracy, hitting his target every time. *'''Bilingualism''': Thor is fluent in his native Asgardian, as well as English. ==Equipment== ===Weapons=== *'''[[Mjølnir]]''': Thor has been entrusted with the mighty hammer Mjølnir, that was forged from the heart of a dying star. Thor's mystical hammer Mjølnir, which resembles a mallet rather than a traditional war hammer, has a number of elemental based powers. It has been stated by [[Odin/Party Prince Thor|Odin]] himself that Mjølnir's power has no equal. Mjølnir itself is extremely durable and combined with the various enchantments placed upon it by Odin, it is, for all intents and purposes, indestructible. Thor often uses the hammer as a physical weapon, with almost nothing being capable of withstanding a hammer blow or throw, with the tough materials such as vibranium like in the case of his [[Thor|original variant]]. Thanks to the enchantment of Odin, an unworthy individual cannot pick up Thor's hammer. This has allowed him to defeat several powerful enemies such as [[Captain Marvel/Party Prince Thor|Carol Danvers]] by simply placing Mjlonir on top of them. ===Other Equipment=== *'''[[Asgardian Armor]]''': ''To be added'' ==Facilities== *'''[[Royal Palace of Valaskjalf]]''': ''To be added'' *'''[[Whip & Fiddle]]''': ''To be added'' ==Relationships== {{Col-begin}}{{Col-2}} ===Family=== *[[Odin/Party Prince Thor|Odin]] - Father *[[Frigga/Party Prince Thor|Frigga]] - Mother *Aunt ===Allies=== *[[Sif/Party Prince Thor|Sif]] - Friend *[[Warriors Three/Party Prince Thor|Warriors Three]] - Friends **[[Fandral/Party Prince Thor|Fandral]] **[[Hogun/Party Prince Thor|Hogun]] **[[Volstagg/Party Prince Thor|Volstagg]] *[[Loki/Party Prince Thor|Loki]] -
